rationale: A suspiciously named executable from the Downloads folder ('ILL_UNBLINKING.exe', EV-001/EV-002/EV-004) repeatedly spawns notepad.exe instances and immediately injects remote threads into each one (A0, EV-003, EV-005) — a classic process injection pattern used to execute shellcode under a trusted host process. The injected thread start addresses resolve to no known module ('-'), indicating shellcode injected directly into allocated memory rather than a legitimate DLL, which rules out benign explanations. The pattern repeats three times within ~3 minutes, suggesting an automated injection loop. The source binary's name, location (Downloads), and behavior collectively constitute strong indicators of malicious intent; no legitimate software exhibits this combination of behaviors.
